channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Lena Dunham nos hacía mucha falta
item.description: La era de “Girls” acabó hace tiempo. Pero su creadora aún tiene mucho que decir y enseñarnos.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Hess

item.title: El gobierno de Trump está malinterpretando la historia
item.description: Como en tiempos de Tucídides, la postura bélica del gobierno de Estados Unidos promete beneficios a corto plazo pero desastre a largo plazo.
item.dc:creator: Stewart Patrick
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 20, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil Prices Rise as Iran Vows Retaliation for Cargo Ship Attack
item.description: Oil prices rose to $96 a barrel after the U.S. attacked and seized an Iranian cargo ship. President Trump said American negotiators were heading to Pakistan for more peace talks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Tankers and bulk carriers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: Qué significa tacit
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 106 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: tacit
item.description: This word has appeared in 106 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
item.description: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A floating fuel station selling diesel last month in the Mekong River delta. Vietnam has been hit hard by rising fuel costs because of the war in Iran.

item.title: Se fueron de EE. UU. para ahorrar dinero. Volver parece inasequible
item.description: Algunos estadounidenses que viven en el extranjero se han dado cuenta de que no pueden reproducir en EE. UU. el mismo estilo de vida cómodo que han tenido en otros países.
item.dc:creator: Will McCurdy

media:content.media:credit: Tako Robakidze for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nino Trentinella has built a new life in Tbilisi, Georgia, where she can afford a housekeeper and regular meals at restaurants.

item.title: ‘Half Man,’ and 5 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: This new series, starring, written and directed by Richard Gadd of “Baby Reindeer,” comes to HBO, and Hulu observes 420 with a cavalcade of cannabis series.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Anne Binckebanck/HBO
media:content.media:description: From left: Jamie Bell and Richard Gadd in “Half Man.”

item.title: Does the Trump Administration Really Want More Teen Pregnancies?
item.description: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.
item.dc:creator: Jill Filipovic

item.title: Quote of the Day: Rohingya Refugee’s Voyage to Seek Better Life Was Almost His Last
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, April 20, 2026.

item.title: Mueren 4 investigadores de un laboratorio de drogas en un accidente carretero en México
item.description: Dos de las víctimas eran personal de la embajada de EE. UU. y dos eran agentes antidroga de México, según las autoridades de Chihuahua.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas

media:content.media:credit: Procuraduría General de Justicia de Chihuahua, vía Reuters
media:content.media:description: Un presunto laboratorio de procesamiento de metanfetamina descubierto en el estado de Chihuahua, al norte de México

item.title: In Turkey, Middle Powers Ponder Diplomacy With a Rogue U.S.
item.description: The U.S. remains an essential player. The problem, one analyst said, is how to deal effectively with a power that is “indispensable, coercive and unpredictable at the same time.”
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Riza Ozel/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey in Antalya on Friday, where he said the global system was in “a moral and existential crisis.”

item.title: No Corrections: April 20, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, April 20, 2026.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 5 Recap: No Captain
item.description: “How’s That?!” needs a real leader. The guys surrounding Valerie right now clearly aren’t up to the task.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:credit: Erin Simkin/HBO
media:content.media:description: Brandon (Andrew Scott) tells Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) that her show is his least troublesome production. That probably isn’t a good thing.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes a Boat in the Caribbean, Killing 3
item.description: The latest attack raised the death toll to at least 180 in the campaign by the United States against people it accuses of smuggling drugs at sea.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, the head of the Southern Command, ordered the strike in the Caribbean Sea on Sunday.

item.title: Poetry Challenge: Memorize “The More Loving One” by W.H. Auden
item.description: It’s about love, the cosmos and everything in between. And all week we’ll have games and readings by poets laureate, beloved authors and an Oscar winner to help you memorize it.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott and Aliza Aufrichtig

item.title: Mexico Car Accident Kills 4 Drug Investigators in Chihuahua.
item.description: Two of the victims were American and two were Mexican, the authorities in Chihuahua said. The accident took place on a treacherous mountain road.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas

media:content.media:credit: Attorney General'S Office of Chihuahua, via, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab discovered in northern Mexico’s Chihuahua state.

item.title: U.S. Museums Reach Deep into America’s Past
item.description: Museums around the country are celebrating the nation’s heritage in ways that go beyond what might be considered traditional.
item.dc:creator: Alina Tugend

item.title: Review: Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara Seduce in ‘Fallen Angels’
item.description: Lust is the comic engine driving the action of a riotous revival of one of Noël Coward’s early plays, with Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara, at Todd Haimes Theater.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times

item.title: Robot Finishes Half Marathon Faster Than Human World Record
item.description: A humanoid robot named Lightning ran a half-marathon race in Beijing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — faster than the human world record for the distance.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso and Zach Wasser

media:content.media:credit: Kevin Frayer/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The 5-foot-5 humanoid robot Lightning, with its approximately 3-foot-long legs striding, showed its form in a half-marathon race on Sunday in Beijing.

item.title: U.S. Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship in Arabian Sea
item.description: The military said it had disabled the vessel after it ignored repeated warnings to stop, amid a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. Marines boarded the ship and seized it.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Centcom, via X
media:content.media:description: A screenshot of a video posted by U.S. Central Command that was described as showing a U.S. Navy destroyer warning and then firing on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Arabian Sea on Sunday.

item.title: Moya Brennan, Whose Gaelic Pop Band Won Worldwide Fame, Dies at 73
item.description: Clannad consisted of her uncles, siblings and, briefly, her sister Enya. In a memoir, Ms. Brennan described a stormy youth followed by a Christian reawakening.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub

media:content.media:credit: C Brandon/Redferns via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Moya Brennan in 2014 performing with the Gaelic pop band Clannad in London. She played the harp, but it was her voice — breathy, with a delicate vibrato — that provided the band’s signature sound.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Attacked Iranian Cargo Ship
item.description: President Trump said a Navy destroyer had attacked and seized an Iranian-flagged vessel in the Gulf of Oman. The White House said it was dispatching a high-level delegation, including Vice President JD Vance, to Pakistan for negotiations.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Races to a Record Half-Marathon Finish
item.description: The android won a half-marathon for robots (and humans) on Sunday in Beijing, achieving a technological milestone while finishing faster than any person in history.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:credit: Kevin Frayer/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The 5-foot-5 humanoid robot Lightning, with its approximately 3-foot-long legs striding, showed its form in a half-marathon race on Sunday in Beijing.

item.title: From Pulpit to Pews, Trump and Pope Are on the Minds of Catholics
item.description: At churches with connections to Pope Leo and the Trump administration, pastors and parishioners speak out on the feud between the president and the pontiff.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight, Robert Chiarito, Kevin Williams and Kyle Bagenstose

media:content.media:credit: Simone Risoluti/Vatican Media, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV called for peace in a “world ravaged by wars” in his Easter message at St. Peter’s Basilica.

item.title: Oil Prices Jumped and Stock Futures Fell on Renewed Iran Conflict
item.description: Traders, hopeful on Friday that a peace deal was near, were digesting the developments over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Asghar Besharati/Associated Press

item.title: Louisiana Father Kills His Children in Domestic Violence Shooting
item.description: A gunman killed 10 people, including his wife and seven of his children, in a domestic violence attack on Sunday in Shreveport, La., the police said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso and Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Jill Pickett/The Times-Picayune, via The New Orleans Advocate, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Police tape blocks off a house in Shreveport, La., that is one of the locations tied to a mass shooting.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: April 20, 2026
item.description: Freddie Cheng’s puzzle brings us into the wild.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times

item.title: Michigan Gas Clerk Helps Save Kidnapped Teen Girl Who Mouthed ‘Help’
item.description: A fellow student who had witnessed the kidnapping called the police, and other students helped track the girl to a gas station.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

item.title: Democrats Eye a Broader Battlefield to Capture Congress in November
item.description: Weighed down by President Trump’s approval ratings, some Republican incumbents are struggling to raise money while Democrats look for targets like a Tennessee seat south of Nashville.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “We need to be a little nicer,” said Chaz Molder, the mayor of Columbia, Tenn., who is running for a Republican House seat and hopes to show that Democrats can break through with rural voters.

item.title: Messy War, Happy Stock Markets
item.description: On the surface, the recent rally doesn’t seem to make sense: Oil prices are high and a peace deal with Iran remains elusive. Here’s what’s going on.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Timothy A. Clary/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Gwendolyn Chisolm, Who Rhymed on Rap’s First Female Hit, Dies at 66
item.description: A chance encounter led to overnight success for the Sequence, a seminal trio whose “Funk You Up” broke barriers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Barboza/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Sequence in 1980. From left: Cheryl Cook (Cheryl the Pearl), Gwendolyn Chisolm (Blondy) and Angela Brown, later Angie Stone (Angie B.).

item.title: Can U.S. Blockade Iranian-Linked Ships Anywhere in the World? Yes, But …
item.description: Maritime and military law experts say an expansion of the naval blockade announced last week raises legal and practical questions but has ample historical precedent.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Asghar Besharati/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Tankers anchored off Iran on Saturday.

item.title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
item.description: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim and Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The price of gas has increased significantly since the war with Iran began in late February.

item.title: Elaine Ingham, Who Taught That Soil Is Alive, Dies at 73
item.description: A scientist and leader in the organic farming movement, she popularized the “soil food web,” an understanding that soil is a complex realm of microorganisms.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

item.title: Harry Keyishian, Lead Plaintiff in Academic Freedom Case, Dies at 93
item.description: He was one of five University of Buffalo faculty members fired for not signing loyalty oaths. In a landmark decision, the Supreme Court ruled in their favor.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: State University of New York at Buffalo

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Cargo Ship as Both Countries Claim Cease-Fire Violations
item.description: President Trump said the United States attacked an Iranian ship that tried to get past the U.S. blockade, hours after Iran said the blockade was a war crime that violated the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporter on Air Force One.

item.title: Hamas Officials Say Group Is Ready to Hand Over Some Weapons
item.description: Two senior Hamas officials in Gaza said the group was prepared to relinquish some automatic rifles and other arms, a concession that falls short of Israeli-U.S. demands.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and Adam Rasgon

media:content.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Armed members of Hamas in Nuseirat, Gaza, last year.

item.title: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan
item.description: The Department of Justice is seeking ballots and other materials from the 2024 election. Michigan officials call it election interference.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A voter is handed a ballot in Woodworth School in Dearborn, Mich., during the 2024 election.

item.title: Don Schlitz, Prolific Writer of Country Music Hits, Dies at 73
item.description: He won a Grammy for the Kenny Rogers song “The Gambler,” and also wrote for Randy Travis, the Judds and Mary Chapin Carpenter.
item.dc:creator: Bill Friskics-Warren

item.title: Haunted by ‘Dark Thoughts,’ Louisiana Father Kills 8 Children
item.description: Seven of the eight children killed were the shooter’s own. Two other people were gravely wounded. The gunman, who was struggling with mental health problems, died in a confrontation with police.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Christina Morales and Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Jill Pickett/The Times-Picayune, via The New Orleans Advocate, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Police tape blocks off a house in Shreveport, La., that is one of the locations tied to a mass shooting.

item.title: British Counterterrorism Police Investigating Attacks Against Jewish Sites
item.description: The police say they are focusing on a shadowy Islamic group that may have links to Iran and which has claimed responsibility for several recent arson attacks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:credit: Justin Tallis/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Police forensic officers worked Sunday inside a cordon set up near Kenton United Synagogue. The synagogue, in Harrow, northwest London, was the scene of an overnight arson attack.

item.title: Fire Destroys Most of Coastal Village in Malaysia
item.description: A community of stilt homes on the island of Borneo was reduced to ash in an overnight blaze.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed

media:content.media:credit: Malaysia’s Sandakan Fire And Rescue Department, via Agence France-Presse
media:content.media:description: A view of Kampung Bahagia, on the island of Borneo in Malaysia, in a handout photo taken and released by Malaysia’s Sandakan Fire and Rescue Department on Sunday.

item.title: 5 Injured During a Shooting Near University of Iowa Campus
item.description: Three students were among those wounded in the shooting, which took place shortly before 2 a.m. as a fight broke out at the downtown pedestrian mall.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A shooting took place in downtown Iowa City after a large fight broke out at the pedestrian mall.

item.title: Salsa macha pork noodles to transport you to spring in Los Angeles
item.description: You don’t have to live in Southern California to recreate the way cuisines come together. Start with these 30-minute spicy pork noodles.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Ko

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Octavio Peña’s salsa macha pork noodles.

item.title: Iran Eases Some Internet Restrictions, as Wider Blackout Passes 50th Day
item.description: Critics say Iran may be creating a “tiered internet” model, where access is limited to the politically and economically privileged.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Two women with their phones in Tehran, Iran, on Thursday.

item.title: Times Are Hard. What’s a Teacher to Do?
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion essay by Frank Bruni. Also: An arch near Arlington; cuts in mental health care; transformation in prison.

item.title: Ageless Beauty
item.description: We examine a surprising trend on the runways of Paris.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Inside the Making of “Love on the Spectrum”
item.description: Inside the making of Netflix’s hit “Love on the Spectrum.”
item.dc:creator: Rachel Abrams, Luke Vander Ploeg, Tina Antolini, Anna Peele, Wendy Dorr, Sophia Lanman, Marion Lozano and Dan Powell

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Madison Marilla and Tyler White, two stars of “Love on the Spectrum.”
